records.ety: [LL. legitimatus, p. p. of legitimare to legitimate, fr. L. legitimus legitimate. See Legal.]

defs: Accordant with law or with established legal forms and requirements; lawful; as, legitimate government; legitimate rights; the legitimate succession to the throne; a legitimate proceeding of an officer; a legitimate heir.
Authorized; real; genuine; not false, counterfeit, or spurious; as,$legitimate poems of Chaucer; legitimate inscriptions.
Conforming to known principles, or accepted rules; as, legitimate reasoning; a legitimate standard, or method; a legitimate combination of colors.
quotes: Tillotson still keeps his place as a legitimate English classic.

defs: Following by logical sequence; reasonable; as, a legitimate result; a legitimate inference.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Legitimated (-mā`td); p. pr. & vb. n. Legitimating (-mā`tng).]

defs: To make legitimate, lawful, or valid; esp., to put in the position or state of a legitimate person before the law, by legal means; as, to legitimate a bastard child.
quotes: To enact a statute of that which he dares not seem to approve, even to legitimate vice.
